topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a familiar, unwelcome intrusion. My jaw tightened almost imperceptibly as I stared at the profile picture: a grainy, overly-filtered selfie from a decade ago, featuring a sneer I hadn't seen in years. It was Mark. Mark, the architect of my early awkward years, the king of playground taunts. A message followed: "Hey. Long time no talk. What's up?" A slow, hot flush crept up my neck. I picked up my phone, my thumb hovering over the block button, feeling a distinct clenching in my chest. *Seriously? After all this time?* I sighed, a long, drawn-out sound that seemed to drain the air from the room. I deleted the request immediately, the screen momentarily blank before I locked the device.

[story 2]

I was halfway through making coffee, the steam curling lazily toward the ceiling, when the chime sounded. I glanced at the screen, and my hand froze mid-air. It was a request from Mark. My shoulders slumped, a heavy, weary weight settling onto my back. I could practically feel the tension coiling in my neck. *Of all the times. Why now?* I leaned against the counter, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, trying to physically contain the rising tide of irritation. The sheer audacity of it made me want to throw the mug across the room. I didn't even bother to read the message; I just navigated straight to the settings and blocked the contact, the action swift and decisive.

[story 3]

The email landed in my inbox with a dull thud. It was from Mark. The subject line was predictably vague. I rubbed the bridge of my nose, the skin there feeling tight and hot. I reread the opening line twice, my eyes scanning the words as if searching for some hidden trap. A low growl rumbled in my throat. *He actually thought this was a good idea.* I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between the sofa and the window. Every muscle in my body felt coiled, ready to spring, yet utterly drained by the sheer predictability of the situation. I stopped pacing, took a deep, shaky breath, and without another thought, deleted the invitation.
